text:
Admission Date:  2008-02-09              Discharge Date:   1983-11-03

Date of Birth:  2001-09-22             Sex:   F

Service: NEUROLOGY

Allergies:
Patient recorded as having No Known Allergies to Drugs

Attending:Lisa
Chief Complaint:
Vomiting, unresponsiveness

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
None

History of Present Illness:
Patient is a 79 year old right handed woman with past medical
history of hypercholesterolemia, hypertension, osteoporosis,
osteoarthritis who was transferred to Sci-waymart Forensic Treatment Center ED via 3749 Dawn Points
Christianville, OR 38413-8926
from Chilton Memorial Hospital Hospital for evaluation of subdural
hemorrhage.

On the evening of 2000-08-20, the patient was in her usual state of
health. After dinner, she appeared "more wobbly than usual" when
walking up the stairs. Shortly thereafter, she began vomiting.
Over course of a few minutes, she became unresponsive to verbal
and painful stimuli. EMS was called.

Upon EMS arrival at 23:25, she was persistently vomiting,
unresponsive. BP 180/palp, breathing 30-40s. Transported to
Chilton Memorial Hospital Hospital. There, she was intubated for airway
protection after Succhinylcholine 100 mg IV, Etomidate 20 mg IV,
Ativan 2 mg. She also received Labetalol 10 mg IV. Head CT
revealed a large subdural hematoma in the high left frontal
parietal region with large subfalcine shift of 2 cm. Tranported
to Sci-waymart Forensic Treatment Center via Med Flight.

On arrival to Sci-waymart Forensic Treatment Center ED, temp 96.4, HR 58, BP 188/98, O2 100% on
vent, RR 14 and not overbreathing vent.

While in ED, received 50 gram of Mannitol and 1 gram of
Dilantin.

Past Medical History:
PMHx:
1. Hypertension
2. High cholesterol
3. Osteoporosis
4. Arthritis
5. Dementia, ?multi-infarct

Social History:
Lives at home with her husband. Jacqueline Walker been more agitated and
confused over the past few months.

Physical Exam:
Tc 96.4  BP 188/98  HR 58  RR 14  O2Sat 100%/vent
Gen - WD/WN, comfortable, NAD.
HEENT - Left periorbital ecchymosis. +ETT, +OGT.
Neck - Hard cervical collar.
Lungs - CTA bilaterally. No R/R/W.
Cardiac - RRR. S1/S2. No M/R/G.
Abd - Soft, NT, ND, +NABS. No rebound or guarding. No HSM.
Extrem - Warm and well-perfused. No C/C/E. Bruise over right
shoulder. Bruise over left shin.

Neuro
Not on any sedation for >1 hour during time of exam. No response
to verbal stimuli, sternal rub, nasal tickle.

Cranial Nerves:
I - Not tested
II - Pupils 6mm and fixed. Blurring of temporal margins of optic
discs.
III, IV, VI - No oculocephalic or oculovestibular reflexes.
V, VII - Absent corneal reflex bilaterally.
VIII - Unable to assess.
IX, X - No gag reflex.
Shawn - Unable to assess.
XII - Tongue obscurred by ETT.

Motor/Sensory - Normal bulk. Decreased tone in bilateral upper
extremities. Increased tone in bilateral lower extremities. No
spontaneous movements. Arms extended at sides. Legs both
externally rotated. Extensor posturing of both upper extremities
to nailbed pressure. Triple flexion response of lower
extremities to nailbed pressure.

Reflexes - 1+ DTR at biceps, triceps, brachioradialis, and
patella, bilaterally. 0 at ankles bilaterally. Toes upgoing
bilaterally.

Coordination, gait - Unable to assess.

Pertinent Results:
Head CT 08-09 - There is a 23 mm thick mixed density convexity
subdural hematoma on the left side. There is extensive mass
effect with a 16 mm shift of normally midline structures to the
right. There is distortion and dilatation of the ventricular
system. There is incipient left uncal herniation. The Harrison-white
differentiation is preserved. The surrounding osseous structures
are unremarkable. A small amount of fluid is visualized in the
right maxillary sinus.

CBC 15.8>34.9<319
Na 124  K 2.8  Cl 88  CO2 19  BUN 15  Cr 1.0  Glu 178
Ca 8.6  Mg 1.6  Ph 1.9
CK 377  MB 12  Trop 0.02
PT 12.2  PTT 22.5  INR 1.0

Brief Hospital Course:
Patient is a 79 year old right handed woman with past medical
history of hypertension, hypercholesterolemia who was
transferred from Chilton Memorial Hospital Hospital
after acute onset of wobbly gait, nausea/vomiting, and decreased
level of responsiveness. Head CT at Chilton Memorial Hospital revealed
large subdural hemorrhage with subfalcine herniation.

On initial exam, there was evidence of brainstem compression
with absent pupillary, corneal, oculocephalic and
oculovestibular responses, as well as absent gag reflex. Minimal
respiratory effort above the ventilator. She had extensor and
triple flexion responses to pain.

She was evaluated by Neurosurgery at the time of transfer. Due
to absent brainstem reflexes at presentation, she was felt not
to be a surgical candidate. The extent of injury was discussed
with her family. Transitioned to comfort measures only status on
2008-02-09 at 7:30pm. Maintenance IVF were discontinued, the
patient was extubated, and a morphine drip was initiated for
comfort. On 1983-11-03 at 2:08pm, the patient was pronounced dead
after cardiorespiratory arrest. Her brother was at the bedside;
her husband was notified via telephone. Due to trauma as a
likely cause of the patient's subdural hemorrhage, the medical
examiner was notified. Plan for autopsy by the medical examiner
on 1996-04-01. The patient's family is aware of this plan.

Medications on Admission:
Plavix 75 mg po qd
Lipitor 10 mg po qd
Altace 5 mg po qd
Norvasc 2.5 mg po qd
Magnesium
Metamucil
Prilosec


Discharge Medications:
Not applicable

Discharge Disposition:
Expired

Discharge Diagnosis:
Left subdural hemorrhage

Discharge Condition:
Expired

Followup Instructions:
Not applicable

                             Brittany Kirk Craig Abbott MD, 79712

Completed by:1983-11-03
